subject: Sports
title: The End of the Ancient Games
description: A 60-second lesson on The End of the Ancient Games.

hook.text: After more than a millennium, the greatest sporting event in the ancient world simply stopped.

buildup.text: As Rome rose, the Games shifted. Roman emperors competed—Nero once 'won' a chariot race he fell out of.

discovery.text: In 393 AD, Emperor Theodosius I banned the Games as part of his campaign against pagan festivals.

twist.text: Earthquakes and floods later buried Olympia under meters of silt. The site was lost for centuries.

climax.text: Archaeologists rediscovered Olympia in 1766, sparking dreams of reviving the ancient tradition.

punchline.text: The Games died with Rome—but the idea refused to stay buried.

quiz.question: Why were the ancient Olympics banned in 393 AD?
